---
title: "Day 1 — Visible Output vs. Stored State"
subtitle: "What the screen shows ≠ what is actually stored"
---

<!-- ============================================================
OUTLINE / SCAFFOLD. Fill each section with GENERIC examples
(reading, value, x) — never the graded speed_mps / flow_lpm data.
Core question: "What does the screen show vs. what is stored?"
============================================================ -->

## The three things a line can do

<!-- TODO: short table. Columns: Line looks like | What it does | Changes state? -->

| Line | What it does | Changes stored state? |
|---|---|---|
| `x = 8.0` | **Assign** — store a value | ✅ yes |
| `print(x)` | **Print** — send text to output | ❌ no |
| `x + 0.5` (alone) | **Bare expression** — notebook *displays* the value | ❌ no |

<!-- TODO: one sentence per row expanding on the table. -->

## The headline trap: displayed ≠ stored

<!-- TODO: explain the "Cell 2" idea — a value shown on screen is not the same
     as a value stored in a variable. Prompt: "Where is the equals sign?" -->

::: {.trap-band}
⚠️ **Watch out** — a bare expression can *display* `8.5` while the variable still stores `8.0`. Screen evidence is not stored evidence.
:::

## Overwrite / reassignment

<!-- TODO: reusing a name replaces the current value; order matters.
     Example: x = 8.0 ; x = x + 0.5  → now x is 8.5. -->

## Non-mutating check

::: {.check-band}
✅ **Non-mutating check** — to inspect state without changing it, use `print(x)` or a bare `x`. Never an assignment.
:::

## Worked state trace

<!-- TODO: 4–5 line generic program, then a trace table.
     Columns: After line | stored x | displayed | printed -->

```{python}
#| eval: false
reading = 8.0        # assign
reading + 0.5        # displays 8.5, stores nothing
print(reading)       # prints 8.0
reading = reading + 0.5   # NOW stored value changes
print(reading)       # prints 8.5
```

| After line | stored `reading` | displayed | printed |
|---|---|---|---|
| `reading = 8.0` | 8.0 | — | — |
| `reading + 0.5` | 8.0 | 8.5 | — |
| `print(reading)` | 8.0 | — | 8.0 |
| `reading = reading + 0.5` | 8.5 | — | — |
| `print(reading)` | 8.5 | — | 8.5 |

## Show your evidence

::: {.evidence-band}
🔎 **Show your evidence** — *trace* the stored value after every line; *predict* what each print shows; *debug* a claim like "the value became 8.5" by naming the exact line where storage actually changed.
:::
